description: Controlled vocabularies for the Sacrifice Law Workbench annotation and analysis pipeline.

source_domains.label: body
source_domains.scope_note: Human or collective body, including parts, integrity, injury, and embodiment.

source_domains.label: disease
source_domains.scope_note: Illness, pathology, contagion, corruption, or medicalized threat.

source_domains.label: wound / injury
source_domains.scope_note: Damage, cutting, hurt, blood, scars, and bodily harm.

source_domains.label: medicine / healing
source_domains.scope_note: Treatment, cure, binding, restoration, or repair.

source_domains.label: birth / generation
source_domains.scope_note: Conception, birth, parentage, descent, reproduction, and renewal.

source_domains.label: family / inheritance
source_domains.scope_note: Fathers, children, ancestors, legacy, bequest, descent, and posterity.

source_domains.label: architecture / building
source_domains.scope_note: Edifices, foundations, pillars, temples, construction, collapse, and repair.

source_domains.label: law / contract
source_domains.scope_note: Oath, obligation, compact, title, right, law, judgment, and covenant-like binding.

source_domains.label: debt / accounting
source_domains.scope_note: Payment, repayment, balance, account, obligation, and exchange.

source_domains.label: religion / Providence
source_domains.scope_note: God, judgment, offense, providence, sacred duty, consecration, and hallowing.

source_domains.label: sacrifice
source_domains.scope_note: Offering, martyrdom, blood, death made meaningful, and loss converted into obligation.

source_domains.label: journey / motion
source_domains.scope_note: Path, progress, course, advance, retreat, passing, and direction.

source_domains.label: experiment / science
source_domains.scope_note: Testing, trial, proof, demonstration, and uncertain political experiment.

source_domains.label: agriculture
source_domains.scope_note: Harvest, soil, cultivation, seed, crop, field, and growth.

source_domains.label: mechanics
source_domains.scope_note: Force, machinery, operation, instrument, and mechanism.

source_domains.label: war / combat
source_domains.scope_note: Battle, arms, field, enemy, defense, defeat, and military action.

source_domains.label: light / darkness
source_domains.scope_note: Illumination, obscurity, blindness, and revelation.

source_domains.label: slavery / bondage
source_domains.scope_note: Bondage, enslavement, mastery, servitude, emancipation, and captivity.

source_domains.label: nature / organism
source_domains.scope_note: Growth, decay, roots, branches, storms, climate, and organic life.

source_domains.label: object / material
source_domains.scope_note: Physical objects, substances, bindings, materials, and their dissolution or manipulation.

source_domains.label: physical destruction
source_domains.scope_note: Crushing, breaking, annihilation, expulsion, and irreversible damage to material things.

source_domains.label: person / agency
source_domains.scope_note: Personhood, individual will, inner strength, and motivated action.

source_domains.label: life / death
source_domains.scope_note: Existence and cessation, vital force, dying, killing, and survival.

source_domains.label: theater / stage
source_domains.scope_note: Drama, spectacle, performance, appearance, and masquerade.

source_domains.label: ritual / religion
source_domains.scope_note: Ceremony, consecration, prayer, sacrament, and religious performance distinct from providential narrative.

target_domains.label: government
target_domains.scope_note: Institutions of political authority and administration.

target_domains.label: political order
target_domains.scope_note: Arrangements of rule, authority, and legitimate governance.

target_domains.label: independence
target_domains.scope_note: Political self-determination, separation from imperial authority, and sovereignty.

target_domains.label: racial identity
target_domains.scope_note: Race-based frameworks of collective selfhood and belonging.

target_domains.label: racial order
target_domains.scope_note: Hierarchy and antagonism framed in racial terms.

target_domains.label: military virtue
target_domains.scope_note: Courage, valor, glory, and honor in military contexts.

target_domains.label: war death
target_domains.scope_note: Dying in battle, the dead, and the representation of soldier mortality.

target_domains.label: imperial authority
target_domains.scope_note: The Emperor or supreme leader as source of legitimacy.

target_domains.label: army
target_domains.scope_note: Military force as collective body and instrument.

target_domains.label: social body
target_domains.scope_note: Society, the collective, or the people conceived as a unified whole.

target_domains.label: purification
target_domains.scope_note: Cleansing, elimination, and removal of perceived corruption or contamination.

target_domains.label: struggle
target_domains.scope_note: Existential or historical contest for survival, dominance, or destiny.

target_domains.label: rebirth
target_domains.scope_note: Revival, restoration, resurrection, and national or racial renewal.

semantic_shift_risk_values.label: low
semantic_shift_risk_values.scope_note: Basic meaning is unlikely to differ materially from present usage for the intended annotation.

semantic_shift_risk_values.label: medium
semantic_shift_risk_values.scope_note: Period, legal, religious, or rhetorical context may affect basic meaning or entailments.

semantic_shift_risk_values.label: high
semantic_shift_risk_values.scope_note: Term requires explicit period-source control before confident MIPVU or CMT use.
